content.problemStatement:
Consider an infinite sequence XiX_iXi​ of nonnegative integers, in which X1=AX_1 = AX1​=A, X2=BX_2 = BX2​=B, X3=CX_3 = CX3​=C, and for i≥4i\ge 4i≥4,
Xi=max⁡(Xi−3,Xi−2,Xi−1)−min⁡(Xi−3,Xi−2,Xi−1)X_i = \max(X_{i-3}, X_{i-2}, X_{i-1}) - \min(X_{i-3}, X_{i-2}, X_{i-1})Xi​=max(Xi−3​,Xi−2​,Xi−1​)−min(Xi−3​,Xi−2​,Xi−1​)

Find the KKK-th term of this sequence.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers A,B,C,KA, B, C, KA,B,C,K — the first three  terms of the sequence, and the index of the term that you have to find.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the KKK-th term of the corresponding sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The sequence is (16,12,9,7,5,4,3,2,…)(16, 12, 9, 7, 5, 4, 3, 2, \ldots)(16,12,9,7,5,4,3,2,…). It can be seen that the eighth term is 222.
